fields.description:
## Why
`test_leaves_registry_coverage.py::test_every_leaf_has_real_body` (`:83-100`) defines "real
body" as ≥3 AST statements OR a regex match for effect-shaped tokens (`client.`, `.append`,
`subprocess.run`, …) over `inspect.getsource`. It passes while a leaf sends the WRONG target,
DROPS a field, returns the WRONG follow-on, or SWALLOWS a required error — a structural proxy,
not behavior. VALIDATED.

## What
Add a per-leaf behavioral effect matrix that drives each leaf in the `_LEAVES` registry with a
mock Jira client and asserts its OBSERVABLE effect: the exact client/store call made (method +
key arguments), the returned `ApplyResult`, any follow-on effects, and its failure behavior.

Completeness (so coverage cannot silently lag the registry): the matrix is enumerated FROM
`_LEAVES` itself (parametrize over `_LEAVES`), and a test asserts EVERY id in `_LEAVES` has a
matrix entry — a newly-added leaf with no behavioral entry fails that test.

Do not blindly duplicate existing coverage: several leaves already have behavioral tests
elsewhere (e.g. `test_apply_outbound_*`). Where a leaf is already behaviorally covered, the
matrix entry may be a thin assertion that references the same observable effect (or the
existing test is folded into the matrix) — the goal is one complete, enumerated behavioral
matrix over `_LEAVES`, not redundant tests. The AST heuristic, if kept, is RELABELED as an
explicitly-supplemental structural guard (not counted as behavioral coverage).

## Scope
- `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/orchestrate/test_leaves_registry_coverage.py` (and/or a new
  per-leaf matrix module) — the behavioral matrix + the `_LEAVES`-completeness test.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The behavioral matrix is enumerated from `_LEAVES`; a test asserts every `_LEAVES` id has
      a matrix entry (a new uncovered leaf fails it).
- [ ] Each leaf's entry asserts its observable effect via a mock Jira client: the exact
      client/store call (method + key args), the returned `ApplyResult`, follow-ons, and
      failure behavior.
- [ ] PROVING STEP (demonstrated, not assumed): for at least one representative leaf, injecting
      a defect — wrong target entity, a dropped field, or a wrong follow-on — makes that leaf's
      behavioral test FAIL; this mutation is shown to go RED and then reverted. (This is what
      demonstrates the matrix actually catches the named failure modes; `make test` green alone
      is necessary but not sufficient.)
- [ ] If the AST heuristic is retained, it is relabeled as a supplemental structural guard, not
      behavioral coverage.
- [ ] `make lint && make test` green.
